# MaiaEdge Call Engagement Schema & Properties

> Last updated: March 2026
> **This is the operational reference for analyzing HubSpot call engagements -- summaries, transcripts, and activity tracking.**

---

## Call Object Overview

**HubSpot Object Type:** `CALL` (engagement type)
**Total Calls:** 113+ as of March 2026
**Primary Data Source:** AI-generated call summaries (`hs_call_summary`) are the richest source of call intelligence. These are detailed, structured summaries auto-generated by HubSpot's AI from call recordings.

---

## Call Properties -- Complete Reference

### Core Call Properties

| Property | Internal Name | Type | Description |
|----------|--------------|------|-------------|
| Call Title | `hs_call_title` | Text | Title/subject of the call |
| Call Summary | `hs_call_summary` | Rich Text (HTML) | AI-generated call summary -- **primary intelligence source** |
| Call Notes | `hs_call_body` | Text | Manually entered call notes |
| Has Transcript | `hs_call_has_transcript` | Boolean | Whether a full transcript exists (`"true"` / `"false"` as strings) |
| Tracked Terms | `hs_call_transcript_tracked_terms` | Text | Keywords detected in transcript (configured in HubSpot) |
| Recording URL | `hs_call_recording_url` | URL | Link to call recording (requires HubSpot authentication to access) |
| Call Direction | `hs_call_direction` | Enum | `INBOUND` or `OUTBOUND` |
| Call Duration | `hs_call_duration` | Number | Duration in milliseconds |
| Call Status | `hs_call_status` | Enum | Call completion status |
| Call Disposition | `hs_call_disposition` | Enum | Call outcome/result |
| Activity Date | `hs_timestamp` | DateTime | When the call occurred |
| Created Date | `hs_createdate` | DateTime | When the record was created |
| Call Owner | `hubspot_owner_id` | Owner | Who logged/owns the call |

### Call Summary Format

The `hs_call_summary` field contains **HTML-formatted** content, NOT plain text. Typical structure:

```html
<h5>Summary</h5>
<ul>
  <li><b>Topic:</b> Description of what was discussed</li>
  <li><b>Key Point:</b> Important detail from the call</li>
</ul>
<h5>Key Notes</h5>
<ul>
  <li>Detailed note about discussion point</li>
  <li>Action items or follow-ups mentioned</li>
</ul>
<h5>Topics Discussed</h5>
<ul>
  <li>Topic category 1</li>
  <li>Topic category 2</li>
</ul>
```

**When parsing call summaries:** Strip HTML tags or parse the structure to extract Summary, Key Notes, and Topics Discussed sections separately.

---

## Call Associations

Calls associate with multiple HubSpot objects:

| Association | Relationship | Use Case |
|-------------|-------------|----------|
| Call -> Company | Many-to-one | Which company was this call about |
| Call -> Contact | Many-to-many | Who was on the call |
| Call -> Deal | Many-to-one | Which deal is this call related to |
| Call -> Ticket (POC) | Many-to-one | Which POC ticket is this call related to |

**Querying associated objects:** Use HubSpot MCP's `search_crm_objects` with objectType `CALL`, then retrieve associations to get linked companies, contacts, deals, and tickets.

---

## MEDDPICC and Call Transcripts -- Critical Rule

HubSpot's smart properties auto-populate MEDDPICC fields on the deal **only from the FIRST call transcript**. After that first call, MEDDPICC smart properties require a manual re-run to update.

### When analyzing MEDDPICC from calls:

1. **Single transcript on contact:** Safe to use the deal-level MEDDPICC properties directly (`buying_process_meddpicc`, `identified_pain_meddpicc`, etc. -- see deals-schema.md)
2. **Multiple transcripts on contact:** Do NOT rely on deal-level MEDDPICC properties -- they may be stale from the first call. Instead:
   - Pull the **most recent** call transcript/summary
   - Extract MEDDPICC insights directly from the call summary text
   - Compare against deal-level MEDDPICC to identify what has changed

### Always use the most recent transcript for:
- Current pain points and priorities
- Updated stakeholder map
- Competitive landscape changes
- Buying process evolution
- Deal status and momentum

---

## Call Owner to Rep Mapping

See `territory-model.md` for complete owner IDs, territory assignments, and state-to-owner mapping. Filter calls by `hubspot_owner_id` to get per-rep data.

---

## Activity Gate (Engagement Health)

Engagement health is assessed using **both outbound activity (our calls/emails) AND inbound signals (prospect responses)**. A one-sided conversation is a risk signal even if we're actively reaching out.

### Outbound Activity Recency

Check the most recent call OR email from our side to the associated company:

| Last Outbound | Status | Action |
|---------------|--------|--------|
| Within 14 days | ACTIVE | Account is engaged -- do not trigger re-engagement |
| 15-30 days | WARNING | Flag for follow-up -- engagement cooling |
| 31-60 days | CAUTION | Needs attention -- schedule call or outreach |
| 60+ days | STALE | Re-engagement required -- may need new approach |

### Inbound Response Signals (Momentum Risk)

Check for responses FROM contacts at the prospect company. Flag accounts where we are actively selling but the prospect has gone quiet:

| Signal | Risk Level | What It Means |
|--------|-----------|---------------|
| Last inbound response 14+ days ago despite 2+ outbound attempts | COOLING | Prospect may be losing interest or evaluating alternatives |
| Last inbound response 30+ days ago | GOING DARK | Prospect is disengaging -- escalate or change approach |
| No inbound response ever recorded | NO ENGAGEMENT | Outreach is not landing -- re-evaluate contact/angle |
| Outbound active but inbound ratio < 1:3 | ONE-SIDED | We're talking at them, not with them |

**How to check inbound signals:**
- Query emails (objectType: `EMAIL`) associated with the company, filter for `hs_email_direction` = `INCOMING_EMAIL`
- Query calls with `hs_call_direction` = `INBOUND`
- Compare most recent inbound timestamp against most recent outbound timestamp
- Flag the delta if outbound is recent but inbound is stale

### Combined Engagement Score

The activity gate should consider both dimensions:

| Outbound | Inbound | Assessment |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Active (14 days) | Active (14 days) | HEALTHY -- two-way engagement |
| Active (14 days) | Stale (30+ days) | AT RISK -- we're active but they're not responding |
| Stale (30+ days) | Active (14 days) | INBOUND OPPORTUNITY -- they're reaching out but we're not following up |
| Stale (30+ days) | Stale (30+ days) | DEAD -- no engagement from either side |

---

## Querying Calls via HubSpot MCP

### Search for recent calls
```
search_crm_objects:
  objectType: CALL
  filterGroups: [{ filters: [{ propertyName: "hs_timestamp", operator: "GTE", value: "<epoch_ms>" }] }]
  properties: ["hs_call_title", "hs_call_summary", "hs_call_body", "hs_call_has_transcript", "hs_timestamp", "hubspot_owner_id", "hs_call_direction", "hs_call_duration"]
  limit: 100
```

### Filter by owner (rep)
```
filters: [{ propertyName: "hubspot_owner_id", operator: "EQ", value: "<owner_id>" }]
```

### Get associated objects inline (avoid N+1)
Include associations in the search query to avoid per-call lookups:
```
search_crm_objects:
  objectType: CALL
  associations: ["COMPANY", "DEAL", "CONTACT", "TICKET"]
  ...
```
This returns associated object IDs inline with each call result. Only use separate `get_crm_objects` calls when you need full property details for a specific associated record.

### Pagination
HubSpot search returns max 100 results per request. With 113+ calls in the system, most queries will require pagination:
1. Check the response for a `paging.next.after` cursor
2. If present, make additional requests with `after: <cursor>` until no more pages remain
3. Always paginate when the result set could exceed 100
4. For aggregate/counting queries, consider fetching lightweight properties first (omit `hs_call_summary`) and only fetch full summaries for calls that need content analysis

### Optimized property sets by use case
- **Counting/aggregation:** `hs_timestamp, hubspot_owner_id, hs_call_has_transcript, hs_call_duration, hs_call_direction`
- **Content analysis:** Add `hs_call_title, hs_call_summary, hs_call_body`
- **Full detail:** Add `hs_call_transcript_tracked_terms, hs_call_recording_url, hs_call_status, hs_call_disposition`

### Reuse fetched data
If you have already fetched call data earlier in the same conversation, reuse it rather than re-querying HubSpot. Only re-fetch if the user requests a different date range or the data is from a prior conversation.
